name: Brañas de la Seita, Zaramedo, and Degollada Loop from Palacios del Sil
name_original: Braña de la Degollada, Zarameo y de la Seita – trail running circular desde Cuevas del Sil (Espacio Natural Alto Sil)
description: This difficult 9.7-mile trail running route in Alto Sil offers spectacular mountain views and visits traditional shepherd's huts.
region_intro: Jogging around Palacios Del Sil offers routes through a mountainous landscape characterized by river valleys, forested slopes, and elevated terrain. The region features numerous hills and peaks, providing challenging ascents and descents for runners. Many running routes traverse natural areas, including those with lakes and open vistas. Best jogging routes around Palacios Del Sil The most popular jogging route is Laguna de San Pedro Mallo – running circular from Matarrosa del Sil by the Cerro de la Laguna, a 6.7 miles (10.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 22 minutes to complete. This circular route offers views of the Laguna de San Pedro Mallo and surrounding hills. Another top favourite among local runners is El Miro de Rabón y Pico Nevadín – trail running circular desde Rabanal de Arriba (Espacio Natural de los Ancares Leoneses y Alto Sil), a difficult 9.9 miles (16.0 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain through the Ancares Leoneses and Alto Sil natural area. Local runners also love the Camino Olvidado: Fasgar Loop, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 30 minutes. Jogging around Palacios Del Sil is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and forested hills. The network offers options primarily for experienced runners, with 2 moderate and 11 difficult routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 80 runners have used komoot to explore Palacios Del Sil's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Palacios Del Sil?
A: There are 13 dedicated running routes in Palacios Del Sil listed on komoot. The region's mountainous terrain means that most of these, 11 routes, are categorized as difficult, with 2 moderate options.

Q: Are there any easy running routes in Palacios Del Sil suitable for beginners?
A: The running routes in Palacios Del Sil are primarily designed for experienced runners due to the mountainous terrain and significant elevation changes. There are no routes currently categorized as easy; the available options are either moderate or difficult.

Q: What are some options for long-distance running in Palacios Del Sil?
A: For those seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24569782" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Fasquedo Llamazal loop from Santa Cruz del Sil&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 22.1-mile (35.6 km) route. Another substantial option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055952216/el-miro-de-rabon-y-pico-nevadin-trail-running-circular-desde-rabanal-de-arriba-espacio-natural-de-los-ancares-leoneses-y-alto-sil" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Miro de Rabón y Pico Nevadín – trail running circular desde Rabanal de Arriba&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 9.9 miles (16.0 km) with significant elevation gain.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the Palacios Del Sil area?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Palacios Del Sil are circular. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056012446/laguna-de-san-pedro-mallo-running-circular-from-matarrosa-del-sil-by-the-cerro-de-la-laguna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de San Pedro Mallo – running circular from Matarrosa del Sil&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the Laguna de San Pedro Mallo. Another circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052039821/camino-olvidado-fasgar-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Olvidado: Fasgar Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What interesting sights can I see along the running routes in Palacios Del Sil?
A: The routes often traverse natural areas with open vistas and river valleys. You might encounter notable mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5371946" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Leitariegos&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6856440" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Cerredo&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055952216/el-miro-de-rabon-y-pico-nevadin-trail-running-circular-desde-rabanal-de-arriba-espacio-natural-de-los-ancares-leoneses-y-alto-sil" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Miro de Rabón y Pico Nevadín&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, takes you through the Espacio Natural de los Ancares Leoneses y Alto Sil, offering stunning landscapes.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by waterfalls?
A: While not directly on a featured running route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151921" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gualta Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable attraction in the Palacios Del Sil region. You may find routes nearby or plan a visit to this natural landmark as part of your trip.

Q: When is the best time of year for running in Palacios Del Sil?
A: Given the mountainous terrain, late spring, summer, and early autumn generally offer the most favorable conditions for running in Palacios Del Sil. During these months, the weather is typically milder, and trails are more accessible. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any running routes suitable for winter conditions?
A: Running in Palacios Del Sil during winter can be challenging due to snow and ice, especially at higher elevations. While some routes might be accessible, it's crucial to be prepared with appropriate gear, check trail conditions beforehand, and consider routes at lower altitudes if available. Many routes feature significant elevation, making them more difficult in winter.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Palacios Del Sil?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many trails in natural areas, but it's always best to check local regulations for specific routes or protected areas. When running with your dog, ensure they are well-behaved, kept on a leash where required, and that you carry waste bags. The mountainous terrain can be demanding for both you and your pet.

Q: Is parking available near the running trails in Palacios Del Sil?
A: While specific parking information isn't always detailed for every trail, many trailheads in rural, mountainous regions like Palacios Del Sil typically have small parking areas or roadside spots available. It's advisable to research the starting point of your chosen route for the most accurate parking details.

Q: How accessible are the running routes by public transport?
A: Palacios Del Sil is a rural, mountainous region, and public transport options to specific trailheads may be limited. Access is generally easier by car. If relying on public transport, it's recommended to research local bus services to the nearest villages and plan your route accordingly, as some trail starts might require additional walking.

Q: Are there any lesser-known or 'hidden gem' running trails in the area?
A: While all routes offer unique experiences, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055958413/branas-de-la-seita-zaramedo-and-degollada-loop-from-palacios-del-sil" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brañas de la Seita, Zaramedo, and Degollada Loop from Palacios del Sil&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 9.7-mile (15.6 km) route that provides an immersive experience through the region's rugged beauty, perhaps offering a less frequented path compared to the most popular trails.
tips: What a spectacle of scenery! The Zarameo braña offers us impressive views of the valley. It is possibly the best preserved in León, and has 22 cabins. Outside of summer, we will be able to recharge water at the source.
With beautiful views of the Rebentón forest, La Braña de la Seita has 10 cabins. The braña belongs to the residents of Cuevas, which is about 3 kilometers away.
Beautiful bridge that gives entrance to the braña. We must be attentive because it is somewhat hidden.
Picturesque forest track next to the Reguerona stream. This path leads to the Braña de la Seita.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Braña de la Degollada, Zarameo y de la Seita trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as difficult and is best suited for experienced trail runners or hikers with a high level of physical fitness and mountain experience. It involves significant elevation changes, technical terrain, and requires sure footing and appropriate footwear. Some sections are described as highly technical or hazardous.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route features diverse terrain, including mountain trails, general paths, and alpine tracks. You'll encounter natural and unpaved surfaces, some paved sections, dense oak forests, stream crossings, and rocky areas known as 'morreras.' The trail also passes through ancient thickets of yew and holly.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The circular route begins and ends in Cuevas del Sil. While specific parking details aren't provided, as a starting point for a popular trail, you can typically find parking within or near the village. It's advisable to arrive early, especially during peak seasons, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is renowned for its spectacular scenery and cultural heritage. You'll visit several traditional shepherd's huts, known as 'brañas,' including the well-preserved Braña de Zalambo and Braña de la Seita. The route also offers extensive valley views, magnificent panoramic vistas of forested mountain slopes, and diverse riparian vegetation along the Sil River.

faqs.question: Is the Braña de la Degollada, Zarameo y de la Seita trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Espacio Natural Alto Sil generally allows dogs, given the trail's difficult and technical nature, it might not be suitable for all dogs. If you bring your dog, ensure they are fit for challenging mountain terrain, accustomed to long distances, and kept on a leash to protect local wildlife, especially considering the area is a brown bear habitat.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay an entrance fee to access the Espacio Natural Alto Sil?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no entrance fees or special permits required for general access to the Espacio Natural Alto Sil for activities like trail running or hiking. However, always check local regulations before your visit, especially for any specific activities or protected zones within the natural space.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to run this trail is generally during the spring, summer, and early autumn months when the weather is more stable and temperatures are milder. Avoid winter due to snow and ice, and be mindful of potential heat during peak summer. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out, as mountain conditions can change rapidly.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route incorporates sections of several official trails. You will traverse parts of the Camino a la Braña de la Seita, Camino a la Braña de Zaramedo, Camino a la Braña de la Degollada, and the Camino de la Peñalba.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a trail run on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and technical sections, you should bring appropriate trail running shoes with good grip, sufficient water and nutrition, a first-aid kit, navigation tools (like a GPS device or the komoot app), and layers of clothing suitable for changing mountain weather. Trekking poles can also be beneficial for stability on steep or technical descents.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the trailhead in Cuevas del Sil?
faqs.answer: Cuevas del Sil is a small village, and while specific amenities like cafes or pubs directly at the trailhead are not detailed, you can typically find basic services, local eateries, or accommodation options within the village itself or nearby towns in the Alto Sil region. It's always a good idea to check availability beforehand.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the Braña de la Degollada, Zarameo y de la Seita trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers truly spectacular and impressive views. You'll be treated to extensive vistas of the valley, magnificent panoramic views of mountainous slopes blanketed in forest, and the unique cultural landscape surrounding the traditional 'brañas.' The route also provides scenic glimpses of the Sil River and its riparian vegetation.
